description:
You are a data-scientist who has just received a very small network
    traffic sample.  
    The dataset needs a “hostname enrichment” step so that analysts do not
    have to look at raw IP addresses.

    1.  Dataset location  
        • Source CSV : /home/user/datasets/server_access.csv

    2.  Helper reference file  
        • Static mapping : /home/user/resources/ip_hostname_map.txt  
          (tab-separated <IP><TAB><hostname> pairs)

    3.  Expected deliverables  
        After you finish, **two new files must exist** inside the already
        present directory /home/user/outputs:

        a.  /home/user/outputs/server_access_resolved.csv  
            • CSV, comma-separated, **no leading or trailing spaces anywhere**  
            • Column order **must be exactly**:
              ip,hostname,timestamp,bytes_sent  
            • The header row **must be present**.  
            • Each data row must be taken from the original
              server_access.csv, with the hostname inserted according to the
              mapping file.

        b.  /home/user/outputs/resolve.log  
            • Plain text, UNIX line-feeds.  
            • One line per IP in the *same order* they appear in
              server_access.csv.  
            • Exact line format (no additional spaces):
              <ip>,<hostname>

    4.  Quality checks performed by the automated grader  
        The grader will:
        • Verify that the two files exist at the exact paths given above.  
        • Compare the content of /home/user/outputs/server_access_resolved.csv
          against a ground-truth copy byte-for-byte.  
        • Compare the content of /home/user/outputs/resolve.log
          against a ground-truth copy byte-for-byte.  
        • Ensure neither file contains any extra blank lines or whitespace.

    5.  Hints (do *not* print the commands directly):  
        • The mapping file is tab-separated; `join`, `awk`, `paste`, or any
          other standard GNU utilities could help merge the data.  
        • Make sure you keep the original row order.  
        • Creating the /home/user/outputs directory is **not** necessary—the
          directory already exists and is writable by the current user.  
        • Try to accomplish everything in roughly 5–10 commands.  
        • Do not hard-code the full file contents; always derive them from
          the supplied input files.

    When everything matches the specification, you are done.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth files that the grader will look for -->

    <!-- Path: /home/user/datasets/server_access.csv -->
    <!-- (already on disk before the task starts) -->
    ip,timestamp,bytes_sent
    192.0.2.1,2023-10-01T12:00:00Z,512
    203.0.113.5,2023-10-01T12:01:00Z,1024
    198.51.100.42,2023-10-01T12:02:00Z,2048

    <!-- Path: /home/user/resources/ip_hostname_map.txt -->
    <!-- (tab-separated, already on disk before the task starts) -->
    192.0.2.1	api.internal.example
    203.0.113.5	db01.internal.example
    198.51.100.42	cache.internal.example

    <!-- Expected content of /home/user/outputs/server_access_resolved.csv -->
    ip,hostname,timestamp,bytes_sent
    192.0.2.1,api.internal.example,2023-10-01T12:00:00Z,512
    203.0.113.5,db01.internal.example,2023-10-01T12:01:00Z,1024
    198.51.100.42,cache.internal.example,2023-10-01T12:02:00Z,2048

    <!-- Expected content of /home/user/outputs/resolve.log -->
    192.0.2.1,api.internal.example
    203.0.113.5,db01.internal.example
    198.51.100.42,cache.internal.example
